entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cultivated pigeonpea (Cajanus cajan) which is susceptible to pod borer and pod fly

entities.name: POD BORER
entities.description: Helicoverpa armigera, a pest causing high yield losses in pigeonpea

entities.name: POD FLY
entities.description: Melanagromyza obtusa, a pest causing yield losses in pigeonpea

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO POD BORER
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand or repel attack by Helicoverpa armigera

entities.name: ANTIXENOSIS
entities.description: A resistance mechanism where the plant is less attractive to the pest

entities.name: ANTIBIOSIS
entities.description: A resistance mechanism where the plant adversely affects the biology of the pest

entities.name: CAJANUS SCARABAEOIDES
entities.description: A wild Cajanus species used as a pollen donor for pod borer resistance

entities.name: CAJANUS ACUTIFOLIUS
entities.description: A wild Cajanus species used as a pollen donor for pod borer resistance

entities.name: PRE-BREEDING
entities.description: The process of introducing wild traits into cultivated varieties through crossing

entities.name: SIMPLE-CROSS APPROACH
entities.description: A breeding method involving a straightforward cross between two parents

entities.name: COMPLEX-CROSS APPROACH
entities.description: A breeding method involving multiple or intricate crossing schemes

entities.name: BACKCROSS
entities.description: A breeding method where a hybrid is crossed with one of its parents

entities.name: INTROGRESSION LINES
entities.description: Lines produced by backcrossing to transfer specific traits from a wild donor to a recipient

entities.name: UN-SPRAYED FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: Field environments where no pesticides are applied to evaluate natural resistance

entities.name: VISUAL DAMAGE RATING SCORES
entities.description: A method of assessing pest damage based on visual observation and scoring

entities.name: POD BORER DAMAGE
entities.description: The extent of damage caused by Helicoverpa armigera on the pods
